records.ety: [OE. hom, ham, AS. hām; akin to OS. hēm, D. & G. heim, Sw. hem, Dan. hiem, Icel. heimr abode, world, heima home, Goth. haims village, Lith. këmas, and perh. to Gr. kw`mh village, or to E. hind a peasant; cf. Skr. kshēma abode, place of rest, security, kshi to dwell. √20, 220.]

defs: One's own dwelling place; the house in which one lives; esp., the house in which one lives with his family; the habitual abode of one's family; also, one's birthplace.
quotes: The disciples went away again to their own home.
Home is the sacred refuge of our life.
Home! home! sweet, sweet home! There's no place like home.

defs: One's native land; the place or country in which one dwells; the place where one's ancestors dwell or dwelt.
The abiding place of the affections, especially of the domestic affections.
quotes: He entered in his house -- his home no more, For without hearts there is no home.

defs: The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat; as, the home of the pine.
quotes: Her eyes are homes of silent prayer.
Flandria, by plenty made the home of war.

defs: A place of refuge and rest; an asylum; as, a home for outcasts; a home for the blind; hence, esp., the grave; the final rest; also, the native and eternal dwelling place of the soul.
quotes: Man goeth to his long home, and the mourners go about the streets.

defs: The home base; as, he started for home.
Of or pertaining to one's dwelling or country; domestic; not foreign; as home manufactures; home comforts.
Close; personal; pointed; as, a home thrust.
In various games, the ultimate point aimed at in a progress; goal
The plate at which the batter stands; same as home base and home plate.
The place of a player in front of an opponent's goal; also, the player.
To one's home or country; as in the phrases, go home, come home, carry home.

quotes: How home the charge reaches us, has been made out.
They come home to men's business and bosoms.

defs: To the place where it belongs; to the end of a course; to the full length; as, to drive a nail home; to ram a cartridge home.
quotes: Wear thy good rapier bare and put it home.
notes: ☞ Home is often used in the formation of compound words, many of which need no special definition; as, home-brewed, home-built, home-grown, etc.

defs: To proceed toward an object or location intended as a target; -- of missiles which can change course in flight under internal or external control; usually used with in on; as, the missile homed in on the radar site.
To arrive at or get closer to an object sought or an intended goal; used with in on; as, the repairman quickly homed in on the cause of the malfunction.
